quote_of_day.text:
>>26687
none of them really care much about me, mother's side went no contact years ago, father's side relatives kept in touch until my grandfather was alive, they all have their own lives.
I am actually grateful for them for giving me my share in our village land.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of board feature requests moved in early, with Anon proposing the addition of science and paranormal boards. The system held steady for a while. accumulating on the topic.

A scattered front of social interaction sparked in the afternoon, as a mention of a potential meetup., with reactions ranging from openness to skepticism.

This was accompanied by a brief but intense downpour of caste-based animosity and anti-Palestinian sentiment, with one anon claiming low-caste individuals were the real enemy. The discussion climate remained charged. on the topic.

Meanwhile, a cold snap of anxiety settled over a few anons, with worries about college reputation and caste dynamics generating some 30-40 replies each.

A drizzle of Hinduism and caste discussions continued in the background, with some participants clashing over the relationship between the two.

articles.title: Anon Proposes Science and Paranormal Boards
articles.body:
Anon started a discussion by suggesting the creation of a science board and a paranormal board on Indiachan.

The OP argued that a science board would be a platform for intelligent users to discuss scientific theories, while a paranormal board would cater to those interested in related topics.

Another anon responded by questioning the necessity of new boards, citing the existence of underutilized boards like /lit/ and /pol/. A different anon jokingly suggested that numerous boards would be beneficial only if they garnered significant interaction.

The conversation took a humorous turn when an anon sarcastically asked for a 'dhan' (likely a euphemism for a specific type of content) board, prompting a mod to intervene and shut down the request.

The mod stated that there would be no '/x/' board, referring users to an existing '/kama/' board.

Some users expressed support for the idea, with one anon seconding the proposal and suggesting a preference for a maths board over a science board. Others made unrelated requests, such as a 'cams and meetups' board.

The discussion also touched on the idea of introducing an updoot button and reviving old boards like '/biz/', which was previously deleted. A mod dismissed these requests, telling users to focus on their own content.

The thread featured several off-topic and humorous exchanges, including personal insults and dark humor. The tone of the discussion remained typical of an anonymous imageboard, with users freely expressing their opinions and desires.

articles.title: Meetup Mention Sparks Mixed Reactions
articles.body:
Anon expressed a willingness to meet up with others, stating they found some users fine. Anon responded with hostility, mentioning they lived in Delhi and did not want to meet, using derogatory language towards the original poster and others.

Another anon suggested a meetup in relation to a CJP protest. A respondent jokingly declined, saying they would rather be on CNN. Anon proposed planning an Indiachan meetup. A reply disputed that Greater Noida is not Delhi.

Insults and personal attacks followed, with one user calling another 'based aristocratic aryan' and another asking about Bangalore. A respondent complimented someone's appearance. A user's comment about not wanting a meetup was met with brief agreement.

Personal attacks and insults continued, with one user calling another 'glowie' and another making an 'inb4 dalli' comment.

articles.title: Anon Claims Low Caste Subhumans Are Enemy, Palestinians Demand 'Free Shit'
articles.body:
Anon opened the discussion saying Indians are foolish for hating Palestine or innocent Gaza children being bombed. They claimed the real enemy is low-caste subhumans studying in Anganwadis, who allegedly hate upper castes and wish them harm.

Another anon responded by comparing Pakistanis and Palestinians, stating Pakistanis issued apologies and sent donations for relief when India suffered from earthquakes, while Palestinians demand 'free shit' and allegedly attack Indian people.

One respondent brought up Palestinian terrorist attacks against Indians, specifically the hijacking of Pan Am Flight 73, and claimed Palestinians have not apologized for it. This anon also made derogatory remarks about Indian Muslims.

Anon called for violent actions against 'haughty Muslims', suggesting beheading and rape. Another poster argued that subjugating or genociding violent barbarians is necessary for civilization to progress.

The discussion also involved caste-based insults, with one anon labeling the thread as 'another caste thread' and accusing others of being 'low IQ subhumans'.

Anon also made claims about children from Anganwadis growing up to be sex offenders, rapists, and murderers, and expressed disdain for Pakistanis and Palestinians, with some exceptions for certain violent actions against Muslims.

articles.title: Anon worried about college reputation
articles.body:
Anon was set to join a college, but then found out another person from their caste and hometown, referred to as a "gobar," was also admitted.

This person's presence is causing concern for Anon's parents, who fear it will harm their image. One respondent suggested Anon not listen to their parents and attend the college anyway.

Another advised Anon to talk to their parents and force them to agree. Some expressed skepticism about the potential impact on Anon's reputation.

Anon also faced some hostility, with one poster calling them "spineless fag" and another questioning their background.

articles.title: Caste and Hinduism discussions
articles.body:
Anon kicked off the discussion, stating that Hindus have only one caste, which is Kafir. Anon argued that casteism and the preference for fair skin are inherent to Indian culture, predating British influence.

Another anon brought up Savarkar, describing him as someone who wanted to establish a new cultural movement rather than restore traditional practices.

Anon also claimed that the Varna system, which existed in ancient India, was fluid and different from the rigid Jati system that developed later.
